title: Tactile dental implant driver
abstract: A surgical tool for inserting a dental implant into a maxillary or mandibular socket. The tool comprises an elongated housing having a proximal end and a distal end and a drive shaft disposed therein. The distal end has a head enclosing a gear and pinion, the pinion being driven by the drive shaft. The pinion drives the gear so that the rotation of the gear is perpendicular to or approximately perpendicular to the rotational direction of the pinion. The gear is adapted to receive a screw carrying system or an adaptor which is adapted to receive a screw carrying system. A handle affixed to the drive shaft is disposed at and adjacent to the proximal end of the housing. When the implant is engaged with the screw carrying system, the handle is rotated to screw the implant into the socket and secure it to the bone. The tool of the invention also can be used to remove an implant.
claims: Claims 1 . A surgical tool used for affixing a dental implant into a host bone of a patient comprising an elongated housing having a drive shaft rotatably disposed therein, the drive shaft and the housing each having a proximal end and a distal end, the proximal end of the drive shaft having a handle affixed thereon, the handle being disposed at the proximal end of the housing and rotatable relative to the housing, the distal end of the drive shaft is affixed to a pinion which drives a gear in a rotational direction which is perpendicular or approximately perpendicular to a rotational direction of the pinion, the pinion and gear being housed in a head which is affixed to the distal end of the housing, the gear, being adapted to removably receive an adaptor or a screw carrying system. 2. The tool of claim 1 further comprising one or more than one universal joint disposed between the proximal and distal ends of the drive shaft. 3. The tool of claim 1 wherein the drive shaft is flexible. 4. The tool of claim 1 further comprising, and affixed to the handle, flexible rods adapted to apply torque to the implant. 5. The tool of claim 1 further comprising a reversible ratchet mechanism disposed in the handle. 6. The tool of claim 4 further comprising a reversible ratchet mechanism disposed in the handle. 7. The tool of claim 4 further comprising torsion markings. 8. The tool of claim 1 wherein an adaptor is affixed to the gear, the adaptor being adapted to removably receive a screw carrying system. 9. The tool of claim 1 wherein the gear is a face gear which is adapted to removably receive a screw carrying system and the screw carrying system is adapted to removably receive an implant. 10. A method of using the tool of claim 1 comprising inserting a screw carrying system into the adaptor or gear, placing a tip of the screw carrying system onto and in engagement with an implant that has already been positioned in a socket of a host bone, twisting the handles until the implant is affixed in the socket and then removing the screw carrying system from the implant.
